Internal Notes: This release adds folder support to saved replies. Agents and team leads can create folders from Saved Replies settings, then move existing replies into folders. Folders are visible in the saved replies panel when responding to a ticket. Existing saved replies will not be automatically categorised after release. Folder permissions follow existing saved reply permissions.
Customer Value: Agents can find approved responses faster, especially in teams with many saved replies across billing, onboarding, troubleshooting, and cancellation workflows. This should reduce time spent searching for the right response and help teams keep replies organised.
Open Questions / Writer Flags: Clarify whether folder creation is available to all agents or only users with saved reply management permission. Mention that existing saved replies remain uncategorised until manually moved. Screenshots likely needed.

Internal Notes: Customer profiles now include a read-only activity timeline. The timeline shows recent tickets, internal notes, status changes, account updates, and selected support events. The timeline is designed to give agents more context without requiring them to open several previous tickets. Editing timeline entries is not supported in this release.
Customer Value: Agents can quickly understand recent customer history before replying. This may reduce repeated questions and help agents give more informed responses. Team leads may also use the timeline when reviewing escalated customer issues.
Open Questions / Writer Flags: Need to confirm exact activity types included in the first release. Avoid implying the timeline shows every historical event. Mention that the timeline is read-only.

Internal Notes: The ticket details panel has been reorganised to make high-priority ticket information easier to scan. Priority, status, assignee, tags, customer plan, and SLA due time are now grouped into clearer sections. No ticket data has changed, but some fields have moved to new positions in the panel.
Customer Value: Agents should be able to triage tickets faster and spot key ticket information with less scanning. This is mainly a usability improvement, but it may require customers to adjust to the new layout.
Open Questions / Writer Flags: Update screenshots in ticket management documentation. Release note should mention that some fields have moved. Avoid overstating the change as a new workflow.

Internal Notes: A new first response time filter has been added to reporting. Team leads can use it to filter report data by response time ranges and identify queues, teams, or ticket types where initial responses are delayed. The filter applies to reporting views only and does not change SLA calculations.
Customer Value: Team leads get a clearer way to monitor responsiveness and identify support bottlenecks. This supports operational reporting and may help teams improve first response performance.
Open Questions / Writer Flags: Need to explain what “first response time” means. Confirm whether this uses business hours, calendar hours, or existing workspace reporting settings. Make clear it does not change SLA rules.

Internal Notes: This release adds a new “Manage automations” permission. Admins can grant selected users access to create, edit, pause, and delete automation rules without giving them broader access to all workspace settings. Existing workspace admins keep their current access after release. No existing permissions are removed.
Customer Value: This gives admins more control and supports least-privilege access. Teams can delegate automation management without exposing unrelated workspace settings. This should reduce risk for larger support teams with multiple operational roles.
Open Questions / Writer Flags: This is important and should be highlighted clearly. Update permissions and automations documentation. Confirm whether the permission is enabled by default for any existing custom roles. Avoid saying it improves security unless framed carefully.

Feature / Change: Large CSV exports may be delayed during peak usage
Internal Notes: CSV exports with more than 50,000 rows may take several minutes to generate during peak usage. Exports continue processing in the background, and users can continue working while the export is generated. This is a known issue for DeskPilot 2.4 and is being monitored.
Customer Value: Admins and team leads may need to allow extra time for large reporting exports. Communicating this clearly may reduce avoidable support tickets and set expectations for reporting workflows.
Open Questions / Writer Flags: Include in known issues, not fixes. Avoid promising a resolution date. Consider adding a note to reporting/export documentation. Confirm whether users receive a notification when the export is ready.
